questions: Why does page speed matter for revenue?
How do milliseconds impact conversion rates?
qa.answer: Even small increases in page load time reduce conversion rates and revenue. Faster experiences improve engagement, lower bounce rates, and increase add‑to‑cart and checkout completion, especially on mobile.
implication: Set performance budgets; track Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS).
Prioritize critical rendering path; defer non‑critical JS and third‑party tags.
Optimize images (format, compression, responsive) and enable HTTP/2/3 + caching.
Measure speed by template and device; tie to conversion and SEO metrics.

questions: What are practical steps to improve mobile speed?
How can we reduce latency without hurting UX?
qa.answer: Reduce network and execution cost: ship less JS/CSS, lazy‑load below‑the‑fold content, preconnect critical origins, and use CDNs. Optimize fonts and avoid layout shifts.
implication: Audit JS size and third‑party tags; remove or sandbox heavy scripts.
Adopt image CDNs and modern formats (WebP/AVIF).
Implement server‑side rendering or streaming for key pages.
